title: Biocides Regulatory Affairs - Market Leader Company
QUALITY SPECIALIST - PEST MANAGEMENT EXPERT
description:
**LA TUA OPPORTUNITA’:**


l/La Quality Specialist \- PME ha il compito di:

* Monitorare e verificare costantemente la qualità di servizio offerta presso i Clienti della Società.
* Effettuare ispezioni e visite di controllo qualità presso i Clienti in modo da valutare e conseguire il raggiungimento della loro soddisfazione.
* Effettuare visite di ispezione presso i Clienti al fine di consolidare il rapporto con il Cliente e partecipare attivamente alla risoluzione di problematiche di infestazione in essere. Il Quality Specialist deve pianificare mensilmente visite di ispezione compilando il MOD. Quality Checklist in caso di non conformità rilevata e riportare il dettaglio nel sistema ESI web.
* Effettuare almeno due Retrovisite/Audit Day all’anno, finalizzate alla verifica della corretta esecuzione operativa da parte dei tecnici sul campo. Per valutare la conformità degli interventi agli standard qualitativi e operativi aziendali; verificare il rispetto dei requisiti previsti dalla norma UNI EN 16636 per i servizi di Pest Management;
* Visitare i Top Accounts per ispezioni di controllo qualità facendo relazioni/grafici sullo stato del lavoro e sulle infestazioni riscontrate dando suggerimenti per risolvere le problematiche.
* Formare, informare ed addestrare sul campo (attraverso affiancamento) ed in aula (attraverso specifici corsi di formazione) il team operativo (Service Supervisor e Tecnici) e commerciale (Sales) dell’area di competenza per il miglioramento continuo della qualità di servizio offerta (gestione e risoluzione problematiche di infestazione, rispetto delle procedure, miglioramento continuo del rapporto con il Cliente, conformità alla normativa vigente, rispetto delle procedure di lavoro sicuro.
* Formare e informare in aula o da remoto (attraverso specifici corsi di formazione standard o customizzati) i Clienti della propria area di competenza, assicurando la conformità ai requisiti dei discenti e di altri beneficiari, mirando ad accrescere la soddisfazione e lo sviluppo di competenze attraverso l’insegnamento, l’apprendimento o la ricerca (secondo la 21001\).
* Sovrintendere e vigilare sulla osservanza da parte dei Service Supervisor e Tecnici del servizio delle disposizioni aziendali e legislative in materia di salute e sicurezza sul lavoro e di utilizzo dei mezzi di protezione collettivi e dei dispositivi di protezione individuali messi a loro disposizione.
* Collaborare con il Technical Manager per divulgare temi di interesse, informazioni su tutti gli aspetti teorici, pratici e sulla sicurezza relativi all’attività e per informare sulle caratteristiche di nuovi servizi, curare la formazione, la conoscenza e la divulgazione delle istruzioni per la pratica applicazione del servizio di qualità, conforme alle procedure Rentokil e in accordo con le direttive aziendali.
* Assicurare che lo Standard di gestione minimi per trattamenti Entotherm (RMES \- Rentokil Minimum Entotherm Standard) sia venduto o implementato secondo le disposizioni tassative delle norme e legislazione nazionale, Pink Note, Golden Rules, Registro Globale trattamenti Entotherm autorizzati (GAER), Valutazione del rischio Entotherm (ERA), Procedure operative standard (SOP), Procedure di Lavoro Sicuro (PLS), Manuali operatore delle apparecchiature, Checklist\-Moduli Entotherm.

  

Descrizione dell'azienda  

Rentokil Initial è il maggior fornitore al mondo di servizi per le aziende.


Da oltre 90 anni l’azienda si occupa di Pest Control e Hygiene Services. Attiva in oltre 90 Paesi nel mondo – in Europa, Asia, Oceania, America e Africa – conta più di 57\.000 dipendenti e un fatturato annuo di £ 3\.5 miliardi.


Rentokil Initial Italia offre i punti di forza e l'esperienza di una organizzazione multi\-nazionale, pur mantenendo l'agilità e le caratteristiche di una società locale.


L'attività è focalizzata sulla fornitura di un ottimo servizio alla sua vasta gamma di clienti. La filiale italiana opera oggi con due divisioni: Initial Hygiene, specializzata in servizi per l’igiene, che si è ampliata grazie all’acquisizione di CWS\-boco Italia, e Rentokil Pest Control, che nel 2021 si è ampliata grazie all'acquisizione di Gico Systems, dedicata ai servizi per la disinfestazione e monitoraggio degli infestanti.


Lo staff è composto ora da circa 650 dipendenti, che assicurano la copertura del servizio su tutto il territorio nazionale agli oltre 28\.000 clienti.

  

Qualifiche **COMPETENZE E SKILLS RICHIESTE:**


Siamo alla ricerca di un/una brillante candidato/a in possesso di Diploma o Laurea che abbia maturato un’esperienza di almeno 3 anni nel settore Pest Control/Alimentare/GDO/Farmaceutico e che abbia una solida esperienza nella gestione del cliente (aver lavorato nel settore pest management sarà considerato un plus).


Il/la candidato/a ideale dovrebbe possedere:

* Laurea in Biologia/Scienze Agrarie/Entomologia/Scienze e tecnologie Alimentari/Sicurezza ed Igiene Alimentare, Laurea in Medicina Veterinaria o affini;
* Precedente esperienza in aziende preferibilmente del settore Pest Management/Food / GDO/ Ospedaliero/Farmaceutico Precedente esperienza in team gare per la scrittura di capitolati tecnico\-scientifici;
* Regulatory: Conoscenza UNI EN 16636 / Conoscenza Direttiva Biocidi \- Regolamento UE n. 528 del 2012 / Conoscenza della UNI 11381;
* Forte attitudine commerciale e al dialogo continuo con i Clienti;
* Determinazione e ambizione;
* Capacità di analisi;
* Doti organizzative e Orientamento al risultato;
* Orientamento al cliente e alla qualità del servizio;
* Ottima conoscenza degli standard delle certificazioni quali BRC o IFS o 22001 o Marks \& Spencer;
* Disponibilità a frequenti trasferte nella zona sud Italia;
* Completano il profilo la buona conoscenza della lingua inglese e dei principali strumenti informatici.

  

Informazioni aggiuntive  

Il pacchetto retributivo sarà commisurato all'esperienza professionale nel settore.


Completano il pacchetto retributivo:

* Dotazione di pc e cellulare aziendale
* Auto aziendale ad uso promiscuo
* Partecipazione al piano incentivante
* Ticket Restaurant da 8


La ricerca si intende rivolta ai candidati senza alcuna discriminazione di genere, età o provenienza.


Ci teniamo molto alla privacy dei nostri candidati.  

Pertanto, prima di candidarsi è necessario prendere visione della nostra informativa al link di seguito indicato:  

https://www.rentokil\-initial.com/site\-services/cookie\-and\-privacy\-policy/privacy\-policy/italian\_privacy\_notice.aspx?\_\_hstc\=49426105\.dd62cc660ea46de775918a7402af7235\.1702330099304\.1702330099304\.1702330099304\.1\&\_\_hssc\=49426105\.2\.1702330099305\&\_\_hsfp\=2860141656

title: Security Manager
description:
**Who We Are**


Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world.

**What We Offer**


Location:


Treviso,ITA
You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more.


At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits.

**Role**
--------


The Site **Security Manager** is responsible for leading, coordinating, and enhancing all physical security operations for the company’s Italian site(s). This includes the protection of personnel, facilities, intellectual property, and mission‑critical assets. The role involves managing security systems, supervising security personnel, partnering with law‑enforcement agencies, and supporting regional multi‑site operations. The manager ensures compliance with corporate security standards as well as Italian regulatory requirements.


This position reports to the Israel Senior Security Manager, and is based in Rehovot, Israel. The role requires travel up to 10%.

**Program Components Include**

* Lead security operations for the Italian site, ensuring operational excellence and regulatory compliance.
* Supervise, train, and qualify security personnel, including vendor teams and contracted guards.
* Maintain security systems such as Access Control, CCTV, intrusion detection and related technologies.
* Conduct sensitive investigations and implement improvements to investigative procedures.
* Serve as primary liaison with Italian law enforcement, emergency services, and contracted security providers.
* Lead preparedness activities and participate in emergency response processes

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------


Security Workforce Leadership

* Manage physical security teams, including vendor operations.
* Guide, supervise, and develop security staff, including training and qualification processes.
* Lead risk identification, site assessments, inspections, and audit programs.
* Take part in emergency readiness planning and response execution, including drills and incident reviews.
* Maintain strong liaison with Italian law‑enforcement agencies and regulatory authorities, as well as external contractors and security professionals.
* Conduct sensitive, wide‑scope security investigations at the site or corporate level.
* Provide guidance and operational support to other security personnel engaged in investigations.
* Develop, update, and communicate corporate investigative procedures and processes.
* Establish, manage, and maintain security systems including Access Control, CCTV, alarm systems, and monitoring platforms.
* Ensure system performance, reliability, lifecycle upgrades and vendor support.
* Protect the organization’s physical and intellectual assets through proactive oversight and strict policy enforcement.
* Manage security guards, shift leaders, and supervisors
* Train, certify, and evaluate staff
* Conduct field inspections, drills, training sessions, and operational reviews
* Ensure high‑quality service delivery across all shifts

**Skills, Knowledge, Experience \& Education**
----------------------------------------------

The ideal candidate will have the following:


Strong command of physical security technologies (CCTV, access control, perimeter systems, alarms and more)

* Demonstrates strong conceptual and practical expertise in physical security operations.
* Experience operating and managing 24/7 security teams
* Familiarity with physical security standards and operational protocols
* Strong decision‑making capability under pressure
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Italian and English – Must
* 3–7 years in physical security, with at least 2 years in a leadership role
* Experience managing guard forces, control rooms, security operations
* Proven experience handling physical incidents and producing professional reports
* Experience in industrial or multi‑site environments – advantage
* Security‑related training or certifications – advantage
* Bachelor’s degree – preferred but not required
* Ability to work with technical documentation in English

**Other Suitability Factors**
-----------------------------

To succeed in this role requires a **capacity for complexity** and **temperament** that includes:

* High integrity, professionalism, and reliability
* Strong situational awareness and high stress‑resilience
* Assertiveness combined with strong teamwork and leadership
* Availability for emergencies or off‑hours events

**Workplace:** Olmi di San Biagio di Callalta (TV)

**Additional Information**

**Time Type:**


Full time**Employee Type:**


Assignee / Regular**Travel:**


Yes, 20% of the Time**Relocation Eligible:**


No
Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Sales Development Representative - German & English speaking (remote)
description:
**Company Description**  

At **Quadient**, we support businesses of all sizes in their digital transformation and growth journey, unlocking operational efficiency with reliable, secure, and sustainable automation processes.


Our success in delivering innovation and business growth is inspired by the connections our diverse teams create every day, with our clients and each other.


It’s these connections that make Quadient such an exceptional place to grow your career, develop your skills and make a real impact – help our future\-focused business lead the way in powering secure and sustainable business connections through digital and physical channels.

 **Job Description**  

We are looking for a curious, energetic, and motivated individual who wants to jump\-start their sales career by building the foundation of what it takes to be a top sales performer. SDRs are driven and competitive people, who focus on generating and qualifying new business opportunities for our sales team. We are looking for hardworking employees to discover and pique the interest of VP \& C Level executives.


Successful candidates must have a strong work ethic, humility, and hunger to learn to be part of Quadient, and the drive to not only meet but exceed sales targets. Working closely with our Management team and our Senior Salespeople will give you the opportunity to develop and continue to develop your career in sales. You will learn from the best by working alongside the top performers on the sales team along with sales leadership. Self\-motivated individuals looking to build a solid foundation in software sales are an ideal fit for this position.

**What You'll Do:**

* Manage inbound leads and outbound targeted prospects
* Generate value for potential customers and booking product demonstration webinars and calls
* Managing prospect expectations and overcoming objections
* Reaching and exceed key performance indicators including calls scheduled, calls completed, qualified opportunities and deals won from your meetings.
* Developing skills necessary for promotion into a full, closing role

  

**Qualifications**  

* 1 year of SDR experience, ideally in a software setting
* Fluent German \& English
* A strong interest in building a career in sales
* Curiosity and demonstrable evidence of professional and personal development
* Entrepreneurial mindset \- eager to advance through clearly defined learning and earning paths
* Self\-disciplined, organised and ambitious. Able to consistently hit daily and monthly objectives

  

**Additional Information** **Rewards \& Benefits**

* **Flexible Work:** Embrace a hybrid work model blending office and remote setup for a balanced lifestyle.
* **Endless Learning:** Access global opportunities for growth through our 24/7 online learning platform.
* **Inclusive Community:** Join our Empowered Communities and engage in our Philanthropy program.
* **Comprehensive Rewards:** Enjoy competitive Total Rewards covering wellness, work/life balance, and more, including a generous referral scheme.
* **Caring for Wellbeing:** Access our complimentary employee assistance program for mental health support.

**Smart Work at Quadient**  

At Quadient, our Smart Work approach fosters connection, collaboration, and innovation while offering flexibility based on role requirements. Whether on\-site, hybrid, or remote, our work environments are designed to support productivity and engagement. Hybrid employees balance remote and in\-office work, on\-site roles contribute daily to our vibrant workplace culture, and remote employees stay connected through virtual collaboration and in\-person events. No matter where you work, you’ll be part of a dynamic, people\-first community that drives success together.

**Be yourself at Quadient**  

Our values define how we work as a team: Empowerment, Passion, Inspiration and Community. They inspire us to be EPIC. Together. What makes Quadient different is how different we are. We’re a team of individuals with one goal but many perspectives. When you connect with Quadient, you become part of a community that cares \- in a culture that embraces differences and values every voice.


We will consider any reasonable modifications to the interview process. If you require any assistance with the application process, please email us at career@quadient.com

*Quadient is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. \*: We firmly believe in zero discrimination in employment on any basis, including race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, citizenship status, and any other characteristics protected by local, state, or federal law.*

**People. Connected.**

title: Local Italy Program Leader (Summer 2026)
description:
**Local Italy Program Leader (Summer 2026\)**


Rustic Pathways is a student travel organization that offers the world's finest experiential education, community service, foreign language, and adventure programs. Our destinations stretch from the white\-sand beaches of Fiji to the rainforest canopies of Costa Rica, the high Atlas mountains to the Great Barrier Reef, and many places in between. We provide superior quality travel and service in some of the most welcoming countries for students around the world. Rustic Pathways demands professionalism and integrity across all of our operations, insists on quality in all aspects of our programs, and places a strong emphasis on the protection, safety, and well\-being of our students and staff. We empower students through innovative and responsible travel experiences to positively impact lives and communities around the world.

**Local Italy Program Leader** at Rustic Pathways

**The Position**


As a Local Program Leader, you live in the country of operation, Italy. Your primary responsibility is the care and well\-being of our students while they travel. You are a primary responder in all escalated situations, incidents, and emergencies. In partnership with your co\-leaders, you’ll collaborate to create a safe and successful program experience for all Rustic Pathways students. You’ll work directly with international leaders, community partners, activity operators, and Rustic Pathways managers. You are a liaison between the program experience and all stakeholders, providing trip updates and managing communication with parents and the global Rustic Pathways team. Expect to spend long days in some of the most welcoming communities on the planet. Community service professionals, customer service experts, globally conscious travelers, logistics lovers, and youth development facilitators are encouraged to apply. Rustic Pathways is committed to increasing diversity in our staff and recognizes the benefit of having a diverse, inclusive work environment. We actively encourage those from equity\-seeking groups to apply. We believe in the value of diverse perspectives and experiences represented by our leaders.


Must be safety\-oriented, with a passion for experiential education. The position requires a lot of physical and emotional stamina.


This is a seasonal position.

**Responsibilities**

* Embody Rustic Pathways’ mission, vision, and values
* Act as a role model and mentor to students
* Protect the safety of students and children by abiding by our zero\-tolerance policy for inappropriate behavior
* Monitor and manage student well\-being
* Follow Rustic Pathways’ protocols while on the program
* Address any student health issues and communicate action plans with parents
* Respond calmly to issues as they arise with a focus on customer service and safety
* Demonstrate responsible leadership and judgment, including cultural sensitivity
* Create positive and inclusive group dynamics
* Ensure students understand the history, culture, and context of the places visited
* Model responsible travel by adhering to Rustic Pathways' sustainability protocols
* Facilitate discussions drawing from your own personal experience and Rustic Pathways' discussion guides
* Serve as the primary communicator with local vendors–including restaurants, transportation providers, and activity partners–using the local language as needed
* Coordinate daily logistics with drivers, guides, and other service providers to ensure smooth program operations
* Support translation and cultural mediation between students, staff, and local partners to enhance cross\-cultural understanding and ensure clear communication

**Job Qualifications**

* **Experience:**
	+ At least 2 years of relevant experience in service\-learning, tourism, outdoor leadership, youth mentorship, customer service, or experiential education.
	+ At least 2 years of experience working with young adults and/or adolescents
	+ At least 6 months of experience living and/or working in an international, multicultural, or cross\-cultural environment
* **Education:**
	+ Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university OR equivalent work experience
* **Requirements:**
	+ Must be 22 years of age or older before your start date
	+ Demonstrated maturity and leadership skills
	+ Fluency in English and Italian
	+ Basic CPR and First Aid certification from a licensed provider
		- *NOTE: Certifications must ONLY be obtained through classroom or a blended online \+ classroom training.* ***Online\-only certifications are not acceptable.*** *Certificates must be submitted to Rustic Pathways at least three weeks prior to the start of Program Leader training*
* **Working knowledge in the following areas and/or roles:**
	+ Adventure Guiding, Outdoor Education, or Wilderness Therapy
	+ Teaching and/or instruction, specifically middle school or high school, in a multicultural environment
	+ Teaching (any subject) to non\-native English speakers
	+ Leadership Instruction
	+ Customer Service
	+ Volunteering with AmeriCorps, Peace Corps, and/or national service, or comparable positions
	+ Sociology or Anthropology
	+ Social Justice, Immigration, or Migration
	+ Marine Biology, Environmental Studies, Conservation, or Wildlife Biology
	+ Public Health, Global Health, or Water sanitation and hygiene program
	+ Mountaineering or Technical Climbing
	+ Community Service Coordinator
	+ Surf Instructor
	+ Soccer Coach
	+ Camp Counselor
* **Preferred Certifications (including but not limited to):**
	+ Wilderness First Responder
	+ Wilderness EMT
	+ Ocean Lifeguard
	+ Teaching Certifications (ex. TEFL/TESL)
* **Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:**
	+ Appreciation for diversity in cultures and experiences
	+ Excellent verbal and non\-verbal communication skills
	+ Patient, enthusiastic, analytical, flexible, and solution\-oriented
	+ Passion for discussion\-based learning and helping students make meaning of their experiences
	+ Understands the value of experiential education and travel for high school students
	+ Understands the value of parental communication for high school travel
	+ Eager to learn from colleagues and enjoys open collaboration and teamwork
	+ Able to maintain excellence under pressure and ever\-changing conditions

**Location:**


Ability to work in Italy for at least 1\-8 consecutive weeks at a time (start date determined by mandatory in\-person training dates, which vary).

**Compensation:**


The weekly rate for this position is between $400 to $500 per week (weekly rate is determined by the number of years of Rustic Pathways program leader experience).

**A Note About Equal Employment Opportunity Questions:**


Though you are not required to answer them, we do encourage applicants to answer the Equal Employment Opportunity questions on our application. We cannot see your answers as we review your application. We only see the data as a full set, not connected to any identifying data on your application. In order to most effectively work to increase diversity, equity, and inclusion at Rustic Pathways, we need the data to know where we stand. Numbers are powerful. Please help us to improve as an organization by answering these questions if you are comfortable doing so.

**Equal Opportunity:**


Rustic Pathways is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex including sexual orientation and gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, protected Veteran Status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

**At\-Will Notice:**


Each employee of Rustic Pathways is employed “at will,” which means either the Company or the employee may terminate the employment relationship at any time, for any or no reason, with or without advanced notice. No individual or representative of Rustic Pathways can change this at\-will relationship absent a specific, written contract signed by the Company’s Chief Executive Officer or designee.
